title: Covariance & Correlation
description: Measure how two random quantities move together — and prove it can't lie. From variance as self-covariance, through the covariance formula and the variance-of-a-sum cross term, to the unit-free correlation coefficient ρ, its [−1,1] bound, and the geometry of three correlated variables.
persona: For learners who know expected value and want the second number: spread, co-movement, and correlation. We build every result on finite, exact-rational examples — no calculus required.
tagline: How do two random quantities move together?

lessons.title: Spread: Variance & Standard Deviation
lessons.summary: Variance is spread around the mean: Var(X) = E[X²] − E[X]².

lessons.title: Covariance: The Leftover of the Product Rule
lessons.summary: Cov(X,Y) = E[XY] − E[X]E[Y] — the residue of the product rule.

lessons.title: Variance of a Sum: The Cross Term
lessons.summary: Var(X+Y) = Var(X) + Var(Y) + 2Cov(X,Y); variances add cleanly only when Cov = 0.

lessons.title: Correlation ρ: The Unit-Free Score
lessons.summary: ρ = Cov/(σ_Xσ_Y) ∈ [−1,1]; grade ρ² when ρ is irrational; Cov = 0 ⇏ independence.

lessons.title: The Correlation Triangle: 3-Variable Bounds
lessons.summary: The PSD-determinant condition fences the third correlation; the equicorrelation floor is −1/(n−1).

lessons.title: Synthesis: Min, Max & the Whole Toolkit
lessons.summary: Order-statistic covariance Cov(min,max) = 1/36, ρ = 1/2 — the whole toolkit, told apart on sight.
